---
title: "Preregistration – Advocatus Diaboli Game Cards"
subtitle: "Printable cards for team activity"
author:
  - name: Felix Schönbrodt
    orcid: 0000-0002-8282-3910
    email: felix.schoenbrodt@psy.lmu.de
    affiliations: Ludwig-Maximilians-Universität München
date: 2026-05-21
format:
  html:
    toc: false
    page-layout: full
    embed-resources: true
---

<!-- Note: This is a ChatGPT-generated copy of the Advocatus Diaboli presentation -->

<style>
@page {
  size: A4 portrait;
  margin: 10mm;
}

body {
  font-size: 11pt;
}

main.content {
  max-width: none;
}

.instructions {
  margin-bottom: 4mm;
  padding: 2mm 4mm;
  border-left: 3px solid #6c757d;
  background: #f8f9fa;
  font-size: 0.8rem;
}

.cards-grid {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));
  gap: 5mm;
  margin-bottom: 5mm;
}

.card {
  border: 1.5px dashed #6c757d;
  border-radius: 6px;
  padding: 3mm 4mm;
  box-sizing: border-box;
  background: white;
  break-inside: avoid;
  page-break-inside: avoid;
}

.objection-card {
  min-height: 30mm;
}

.reply-card {
  min-height: 44mm;
}

.card-head {
  display: flex;
  justify-content: space-between;
  align-items: baseline;
  gap: 3mm;
  margin-bottom: 2mm;
}

.card-tag {
  font-size: 0.7rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  letter-spacing: 0.04em;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  color: #6c757d;
}

.card-id {
  font-size: 0.75rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: #495057;
}

.objection-text {
  font-size: 0.95rem;
  line-height: 1.25;
  font-weight: 600;
}

.reply-card ul {
  margin-bottom: 0;
  padding-left: 1.2em;
  font-size: 0.85rem;
  line-height: 1.3;
}

.reply-card li {
  margin-bottom: 1mm;
}

.page-break {
  break-before: page;
  page-break-before: always;
}

@media print {
  h1, h2 {
    break-after: avoid;
    page-break-after: avoid;
  }

  .card {
    box-shadow: none;
  }

  .print-start {
    break-before: page;
    page-break-before: always;
  }
}
</style>

Use: print on A4 paper at 100%, cut along the dashed borders, and give the cards to teams. The small labels (`A1`–`A7`) help you match objections with replies.

## Objection cards {.print-start}

::: {.instructions}
Print these cards, cut them out, and hand one or more objection cards to each team.
:::

:::: {.cards-grid}
::: {.card .objection-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">But...</span><span class="card-id">A1</span></div>
<div class="objection-text">... preregistration keeps me from conducting exploratory research!</div>
:::

::: {.card .objection-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">But...</span><span class="card-id">A2</span></div>
<div class="objection-text">... preregistration takes so much time!</div>
:::

::: {.card .objection-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">But...</span><span class="card-id">A3</span></div>
<div class="objection-text">... someone will steal my ideas!</div>
:::

::: {.card .objection-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">But...</span><span class="card-id">A4</span></div>
<div class="objection-text">... what if I (or my students) make mistakes in the preregistration?</div>
:::

::: {.card .objection-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">But...</span><span class="card-id">A5</span></div>
<div class="objection-text">... someone could preregister multiple hypotheses and take the preregistration that fits the data best!</div>
:::

::: {.card .objection-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">But...</span><span class="card-id">A6</span></div>
<div class="objection-text">... people preregister stuff and then deviate significantly from the preregistration. What is it good for then?</div>
:::

::: {.card .objection-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">But...</span><span class="card-id">A7</span></div>
<div class="objection-text">... I am doing qualitative research and none of the quantitative templates fits my way of working!?</div>
:::
::::

<div class="page-break"></div>

## Reply cards

::: {.instructions}
These can be used as answer cards, instructor cards, or a separate matching set.
:::

:::: {.cards-grid}
::: {.card .reply-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">Reply</span><span class="card-id">A1</span></div>

- Improving your confirmatory research does not mean that you have to refrain from doing exploratory research.
- You can also preregister your exploratory research (e.g., planned analysis methods).
:::

::: {.card .reply-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">Reply</span><span class="card-id">A2</span></div>

- Actually it might even save you time during data analysis and interpretation.
- Preregistration mostly changes the order of the research process (e.g., think about how to analyze your data before rather than after you collect them).
:::

::: {.card .reply-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">Reply</span><span class="card-id">A3</span></div>

- On the contrary, by preregistering your ideas you mark them as yours.
- If anyone still dares to steal them, it becomes much easier to point this out as scientific misconduct.
- On some repositories (e.g., OSF), you can put an embargo on the preregistration so that it is not publicly visible for up to four years.
:::

::: {.card .reply-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">Reply</span><span class="card-id">A4</span></div>

- Everyone makes mistakes, so there is no need to be embarrassed.
- You can always deviate from your preregistered analysis if you provide a good justification.
- If your preregistration is open and someone finds a mistake, that can save you from discovering it too late.
:::

::: {.card .reply-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">Reply</span><span class="card-id">A5</span></div>

- That would be relatively easy-to-spot scientific misconduct compared with not-so-easy-to-spot HARKing without preregistration.
- If more than one hypothesis is preregistered, you would need a lot of luck to have a “perfect-fit” preregistration.
:::

::: {.card .reply-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">Reply</span><span class="card-id">A6</span></div>

- As a reviewer, it should make you more vigilant about the quality of the paper and lead you to require justifications for deviations from the preregistration.
- As a reader, you can better judge the quality of the results by seeing whether they come from confirmatory or exploratory research.
- It helps you spot questionable research practices.
:::

::: {.card .reply-card}
<div class="card-head"><span class="card-tag">Reply</span><span class="card-id">A7</span></div>

- The fact that existing templates do not fit perfectly does not mean that you cannot conduct a preregistration.
- Try to explain: What are your hypotheses? How do you want to investigate them? What will your data look like? How do you plan to evaluate the data?
- If you minimize degrees of freedom, you have met the core goal.
:::
::::
